root_text.an3.72:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā ānando kosambiyaṁ viharati ghositārāme.
root_text.an3.72:1.2: Atha kho aññataro ājīvakasāvako gahapati yenāyasmā ānando tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so ājīvakasāvako gahapati āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.72:2.4: “Tena hi, gahapati, taññevettha paṭipucchissāmi, yathā te khameyya tathā naṁ byākareyyāsi.
root_text.an3.72:2.6: ye rāgassa pahānāya dhammaṁ desenti, dosassa pahānāya dhammaṁ desenti, mohassa pahānāya dhammaṁ desenti, tesaṁ dhammo svākkhāto no vā?
root_text.an3.72:2.8: “Ye, bhante, rāgassa pahānāya dhammaṁ desenti, dosassa pahānāya dhammaṁ desenti, mohassa pahānāya dhammaṁ desenti, tesaṁ dhammo svākkhāto.
root_text.an3.72:3.2: ye rāgassa pahānāya paṭipannā, dosassa pahānāya paṭipannā, mohassa pahānāya paṭipannā, te loke suppaṭipannā no vā?
root_text.an3.72:3.4: “Ye, bhante, rāgassa pahānāya paṭipannā, dosassa pahānāya paṭipannā, mohassa pahānāya paṭipannā, te loke suppaṭipannā.
root_text.an3.72:4.2: yesaṁ rāgo pahīno ucchinnamūlo tālāvatthukato anabhāvaṅkato āyatiṁ anuppādadhammo, yesaṁ doso pahīno ucchinnamūlo tālāvatthukato anabhāvaṅkato āyatiṁ anuppādadhammo, yesaṁ moho pahīno ucchinnamūlo tālāvatthukato anabhāvaṅkato āyatiṁ anuppādadhammo, te loke sukatā no vā?
root_text.an3.72:4.4: “Yesaṁ, bhante, rāgo pahīno ucchinnamūlo tālāvatthukato anabhāvaṅkato āyatiṁ anuppādadhammo, yesaṁ doso pahīno …pe… yesaṁ moho pahīno ucchinnamūlo tālāvatthukato anabhāvaṅkato āyatiṁ anuppādadhammo, te loke sukatā.
root_text.an3.72:5.2: ‘ye, bhante, rāgassa pahānāya dhammaṁ desenti, dosassa pahānāya dhammaṁ desenti, mohassa pahānāya dhammaṁ desenti, tesaṁ dhammo svākkhāto’ti.
root_text.an3.72:5.4: ‘ye, bhante, rāgassa pahānāya paṭipannā, dosassa pahānāya paṭipannā, mohassa pahānāya paṭipannā, te loke suppaṭipannā’ti.
root_text.an3.72:5.6: ‘yesaṁ, bhante, rāgo pahīno ucchinnamūlo tālāvatthukato anabhāvaṅkato āyatiṁ anuppādadhammo, yesaṁ doso pahīno …pe… yesaṁ moho pahīno ucchinnamūlo tālāvatthukato anabhāvaṅkato āyatiṁ anuppādadhammo, te loke sukatā’”ti.
root_text.an3.72:6.2: Na ceva nāma sadhammukkaṁsanā bhavissati, na ca paradhammāpasādanā.
root_text.an3.72:6.3: Āyataneva dhammadesanā, attho ca vutto, attā ca anupanīto.
root_text.an3.72:6.4: Tumhe, bhante ānanda, rāgassa pahānāya dhammaṁ desetha, dosassa …pe… mohassa pahanāya dhammaṁ desetha.
root_text.an3.72:6.6: Tumhe, bhante ānanda, rāgassa pahānāya paṭipannā, dosassa …pe… mohassa pahānāya paṭipannā.
root_text.an3.72:6.8: Tumhākaṁ, bhante ānanda, rāgo pahīno ucchinnamūlo tālāvatthukato anabhāvaṅkato āyatiṁ anuppādadhammo, tumhākaṁ doso pahīno …pe… tumhākaṁ moho pahīno ucchinnamūlo tālāvatthukato anabhāvaṅkato āyatiṁ anuppādadhammo.
root_text.an3.72:7.2: Seyyathāpi, bhante, nikkujjitaṁ vā ukkujjeyya, paṭicchannaṁ vā vivareyya, mūḷhassa vā maggaṁ ācikkheyya, andhakāre vā telapajjotaṁ dhāreyya: ‘cakkhumanto rūpāni dakkhantī’ti; evamevaṁ ayyena ānandena anekapariyāyena dhammo pakāsito.
root_text.an3.72:7.3: Esāhaṁ, bhante ānanda, taṁ bhagavantaṁ saraṇaṁ gacchāmi dhammañca bhikkhusaṅghañca.
root_text.an3.72:7.4: Upāsakaṁ maṁ ayyo ānando dhāretu, ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.

translation_text.an3.72:1.1: At one time Venerable Ānanda was staying near Kosambī, in Ghosita’s Monastery.
translation_text.an3.72:1.2: Then a householder who was a disciple of the Ājīvaka ascetics went up to Venerable Ānanda, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to Ānanda:
translation_text.an3.72:2.1: “Honorable Ānanda, whose teaching is well explained?
translation_text.an3.72:2.2: Who in the world is practicing well?
translation_text.an3.72:2.3: Who in the world has done well?”
translation_text.an3.72:2.4: “Well then, householder, I’ll ask you about this in return, and you can answer as you like.
translation_text.an3.72:2.6: Is the teaching of those who teach for giving up greed, hate, and delusion well explained or not?
translation_text.an3.72:2.7: Or how do you see this?”
translation_text.an3.72:2.8: “The teaching of those who teach for giving up greed, hate, and delusion is well explained.
translation_text.an3.72:3.2: Are those who practice for giving up greed, hate, and delusion practicing well or not?
translation_text.an3.72:3.3: Or how do you see this?”
translation_text.an3.72:3.4: “Those who practice for giving up greed, hate, and delusion are practicing well.
translation_text.an3.72:4.2: Have those who’ve given up greed, hate, and delusion—so they’re cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, obliterated, and unable to arise in the future—done well in the world, or not?
translation_text.an3.72:4.3: Or how do you see this?”
translation_text.an3.72:4.4: “Those who’ve given up greed, hate, and delusion have done well in the world.
translation_text.an3.72:5.2: ‘The teaching of those who teach for giving up greed, hate, and delusion is well explained.’
translation_text.an3.72:5.4: ‘Those who practice for giving up greed, hate, and delusion are practicing well.’
translation_text.an3.72:5.6: ‘Those who’ve given up greed, hate, and delusion have done well in the world.’”
translation_text.an3.72:6.2: There’s no acclaiming your own teaching or disrespecting someone else’s,
translation_text.an3.72:6.3: just teaching what’s relevant in that context. The goal is spoken of, but the self is not involved.
translation_text.an3.72:6.4: You, sir, teach Dhamma for giving up greed, hate, and delusion.
translation_text.an3.72:6.6: You, sir, practice for giving up greed, hate, and delusion.
translation_text.an3.72:6.7: You in the world are practicing well.
translation_text.an3.72:6.8: You’ve given up greed, hate, and delusion.
translation_text.an3.72:6.9: You in the world have done well.
translation_text.an3.72:7.2: As if he were righting the overturned, or revealing the hidden, or pointing out the path to the lost, or lighting a lamp in the dark so people with clear eyes can see what’s there, Master Ānanda has made the teaching clear in many ways.
translation_text.an3.72:7.3: I go for refuge to the Buddha, to the teaching, and to the mendicant Saṅgha.
translation_text.an3.72:7.4: From this day forth, may Master Ānanda remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”
